reasoning: Based on the background text, Trump and Musk are "now fighting on social media" due to Republicans feeling Musk is "overstepping or trying to replace Trump." Historical precedent shows that when Donald Trump feels someone is trying to steal his spotlight, overstepping, or challenging his dominance (e.g., Steve Bannon, Jeff Sessions, Mike Pence, Ron DeSantis), he tends to have very public, bitter, and long-lasting fallouts. Both men have massive, delicate egos and exhibit highly volatile behavior, making a quick reconciliation difficult. While they have mutual interests (Musk needs regulatory favor; Trump values Musk's wealth and platform), Trump's track record suggests personal slights often override strategic alliances in his interpersonal relationships, at least in the short term. Consequently, if they have already had a public falling out by mid-2025, the chances they repair the relationship to be on "good terms" by the end of the year are low, estimated at around 20%.
